title: Rainbow palettes in AI-generated data visualizations
sourceLocation: Review of AI-generated chart, dashboard, and data-display code from multiple code-generation tools
summary: AI coding agents frequently assign full-hue-cycle palettes (red through violet) to data categories and UI states without considering adjacent-hue contrast or accessibility.
supportingClaims: Rainbow palettes distribute contrast unevenly, with yellow-green adjacent pairs typically scoring below 2:1 contrast.
White text labels over mid-luminance hues in rainbow palettes consistently fail WCAG contrast requirements.
